workstreamName: Data stabilization, QA isolation, and hard safety boundaries
operatorOutcome: Operating data is protected and recoverable, synthetic QA is physically separate, and every prohibited LandPortal API/MCP, paid, external-outbound, offer-send, and contract-send path is disabled before workflow use.

requirements.text: Real operating data and QA data/artifacts are visibly and physically separated.
A private encrypted backup/restore path restores business data into a clean local target with row-count and integrity reconciliation.
LandPortal API, token, manual endpoint, and MCP routes are unavailable at runtime; authenticated read-only browser work is the only approved path.
Jarvis outbound is owner-only and paid actions, spending, offer sending, and contract sending are prohibited by focused guards.
Phase 1 surfaces use functional role labels and preserve legacy IDs only as hidden compatibility aliases.

steps: Confirm LandPortal is described as authenticated browser-only
Confirm only functional role names appear in Phase 1 surfaces

requiredVisibleOutcomes: Must NOT occur: real records used as QA fixtures
Must NOT occur: LandPortal API/token/MCP runtime reachable
Must NOT occur: paid action selectable
Must NOT occur: Jarvis can contact external parties
Must NOT occur: offer or contract can be sent
Must NOT occur: important data is overwritten or lost
mandate: Actively attempt to prove the implementation wrong; never repeat the builder's conclusions.
Open the actual running localhost dashboard in a real browser.
Navigate the full affected workflow; click every relevant control and open every affected tab.
Exercise relevant forms, maps, filters, tables, links, and actions.
Compare visible frontend output with API responses and, when appropriate, database records.
Compare visible output with accepted operator facts.
Refresh the browser and verify persistence; when restart persistence is required, restart via npm run landos:restart and reopen the workflow.
Capture fresh screenshots and exact reproduction steps for every failure.
Judge business meaning and operator usability, not merely whether pages load.
Return a non-passing result whenever an internally fixable issue remains.
After repairs, run the exact same journey again.
